source.name: Jennifer Pahlka
source.notes_excerpt: Jennifer Pahlka is the founder of Code for America and author of 'Recoding America: Why Government Is Failing in the Digital Age and How We Can Do Better'. She served as U.S. Deputy Chief Technology Officer under President Obama from 2013-2014, where she helped found the United States Digital Service. She is currently a senior fellow at the Niskanen Center and the Federation of American Scientists

target.name: Recoding America
target.notes_excerpt: Book by Jennifer Pahlka (2023) diagnosing why government technology systems fail and outlining how modernizing digital infrastructure is essential for effective public services and democratic accountability.

target.name: Dan Hendrycks
target.notes_excerpt: Dan Hendrycks (born 1994 or 1995) is an American machine learning researcher who serves as the director of the Center for AI Safety, a nonprofit research organization based in San Francisco, California. evidence: Amodei was VP Research at OpenAI before founding Anthropic

source.name: Dario Amodei
source.notes_excerpt: Dario Amodei (born 1983) is an American artificial intelligence researcher and entrepreneur who co-founded Anthropic in 2021 with his sister Daniela Amodei. He previously served as vice president of research at OpenAI, where he led the development of large language models like GPT-2 and GPT-3. Amodei earned his doctorate degree in biophysics from Princeton University as a Hertz Fellow, and was a p

target.name: Sam Altman
target.notes_excerpt: Sam Altman is the CEO of OpenAI, the artificial intelligence research organization that developed ChatGPT. He became CEO of OpenAI in 2019 after co-founding the organization in 2015. In November 2023, he was briefly fired by OpenAI's board of directors but was reinstated five days later. His net worth was estimated at US$3.3 billion by Forbes in March 2026.

source.name: Casey Newton
source.notes_excerpt: Casey Newton is the founder and editor of Platformer, an independent publication devoted to exploring the intersection of technology and democracy. He co-hosts Hard Fork, a podcast about the future that is already here, alongside a colleague at The New York Times. Prior to founding Platformer in 2020, Casey spent 10 years covering Silicon Valley for The Verge, CNET, and the San Francisco Chronicle
